rationale: RED-on-revert lens holds for all 3 new subtests. (1) present-null refutation_pending_entries: the injected getMeta returns map{key: nil} — the present-null case; reverting the comma-ok guard makes decodePendingEntries(nil) return (nil,nil), CheckPendingObligations then returns nil, so err==nil trips the subtest's t.Fatal → FAIL. Asserts a NON-NIL error plus message contains 'present-but-null'. (2) absent key: getMeta returns only some_other_key; comma-ok present==false falls through → decode(nil)→empty→nil; asserts nil correctly, preserving the §6 no-op. (3) present-null panel_refuted_entries: pending carries a real [X,1] entry (decodes fine) then refuted is nil; on revert decodeRefutations(nil)→empty→[X,1] uncovered→error, but with the WRONG message, so strings.Contains(err,'present-but-null') fails → RED via message. Modeling is faithful: production GetMetadata (bdcli.go:313) json.Unmarshals into map[string]interface{}; encoding/json surfaces a JSON null as a present key with nil value, copied verbatim by the merge loop — byte-identical to the map literal. All 12 subtests PASS (go test -count=1 -v). git clean (only my untracked reviews dir).

findings.note: Subtest 1 & 3 are genuinely RED-on-revert (Fatal on err==nil; message-Contains discriminates on the covered/uncovered path). Subtest 2 correctly asserts nil, pinning the absent-key no-op the fix must not break.
Present-null modeling verified faithful vs production GetMetadata json.Unmarshal → map key present with nil value; comma-ok idiom sees the same shape the test injects.
